title: quercus × urticifolia
intro: Quercus × urticifolia is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between the white oak (Quercus alba) and the stinging oak (Quercus laevis), characterized by its unique leaf shape and growth habit. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Quercus × urticifolia include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Quercus × urticifolia can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for oral consumption, as well as topical creams and salves for skin applications.
